text: Last summer, I planned a trip to Japan, which was both exciting and challenging. Before leaving, I spent weeks researching places to visit and learning a few basic phrases. Experiencing a new culture was amazing—I visited ancient temples and joined a traditional tea ceremony. The food was unforgettable, from fresh sushi to warm bowls of ramen. However, language barriers made simple tasks like ordering food or asking for directions difficult. Despite this, the memories I made—exploring busy streets and meeting friendly locals—are priceless. This journey taught me that travel isn't just about seeing new places; it's about growing through every experience.
